text:
One morning, Tim woke up feeling very excited. Today was a school day.

At school, Tim went straight to reading class. Everything seemed fine at first. Nobody wanted to be Tim's friend. Tim sat alone and felt very sad.
Tim walked home slowly. Tim did not want to go to school ever again.

Anna put on Anna's favourite shirt. It was time for school.

When Anna arrived at school, the class had already started art class. Anna got every answer wrong and the teacher shook her head. Anna felt ashamed.
Anna did not talk on the way home. School did not feel like a good place.

The sun was shining when Sam walked to school with a big smile.

The teacher smiled at everyone as they sat down for math class. The other children laughed at Sam's painting. Sam wanted to disappear.
Sam told Sam's mum everything. That night Sam could not sleep.

Lucy packed Lucy's bag carefully. Lucy could not wait for school.

Lucy found a seat near the window and waited for the playground to begin. Lucy tripped and fell in front of everyone. They all stared. Lucy cried.
Lucy looked out the window on the bus and felt very alone.

It was Monday morning and Jake was happy to go to school.

At school, Jake tried very hard during lunch. The teacher put Jake's drawing in the bin. Jake felt terrible.
When Jake got home, Jake sat in the corner and said nothing.

One morning, Mia woke up feeling very excited. Today was a school day.

At school, Mia went straight to the classroom. Everything seemed fine at first. Mia forgot all the words to the song. Everyone giggled. Mia turned red.
Mia walked home slowly. Mia did not want to go to school ever again.

Ben put on Ben's favourite shirt. It was time for school.

When Ben arrived at school, the class had already started music class. Nobody picked Ben for the team. Ben sat on the bench alone.
Ben did not talk on the way home. School did not feel like a good place.

The sun was shining when Zoe walked to school with a big smile.

The teacher smiled at everyone as they sat down for gym class. Zoe could not read the words on the board. The class stared. Zoe felt small.
Zoe told Zoe's mum everything. That night Zoe could not sleep.

Leo packed Leo's bag carefully. Leo could not wait for school.

Leo found a seat near the window and waited for the library to begin. The teacher said Leo's story was not good enough. Leo felt like giving up.
Leo looked out the window on the bus and felt very alone.

It was Monday morning and Ella was happy to go to school.

At school, Ella tried very hard during science class. Ella spilled paint on the floor and the teacher frowned all day.
When Ella got home, Ella sat in the corner and said nothing.

One morning, Max woke up feeling very excited. Today was a school day.

At school, Max went straight to reading class. Everything seemed fine at first. At lunch Max had nowhere to sit. Max ate alone by the window.
Max walked home slowly. Max did not want to go to school ever again.

Lily put on Lily's favourite shirt. It was time for school.

When Lily arrived at school, the class had already started art class. Lily's project broke apart in front of everyone. Lily went home very sad.
Lily did not talk on the way home. School did not feel like a good place.

The sun was shining when Tom walked to school with a big smile.

The teacher smiled at everyone as they sat down for math class. The teacher gave Tom a bad mark and said to try harder. Tom cried on the bus.
Tom told Tom's mum everything. That night Tom could not sleep.

Emma packed Emma's bag carefully. Emma could not wait for school.

Emma found a seat near the window and waited for the playground to begin. The other children whispered about Emma all day. Emma felt very lonely.
Emma looked out the window on the bus and felt very alone.

It was Monday morning and Jack was happy to go to school.

At school, Jack tried very hard during lunch. Nobody clapped for Jack's poem. Jack wished Jack had never come to school.
When Jack got home, Jack sat in the corner and said nothing.

At school, Jake tried very hard during lunch. The teacher put Jake's drawing in the bin. Jake felt terrible.

One morning, Mia woke up feeling very excited. Today was a school day.

At school, Mia went straight to the classroom. Everything seemed fine at first. Mia forgot all the words to the song. Everyone giggled. Mia turned red.
Mia walked home slowly. Mia did not want to go to school ever again.

Ben put on Ben's favourite shirt. It was time for school.

When Ben arrived at school, the class had already started music class. Nobody picked Ben for the team. Ben sat on the bench alone.
Ben did not talk on the way home. School did not feel like a good place.

The sun was shining when Zoe walked to school with a big smile.

The teacher smiled at everyone as they sat down for gym class. Zoe could not read the words on the board. The class stared. Zoe felt small.
Zoe told Zoe's mum everything. That night Zoe could not sleep.

Leo packed Leo's bag carefully. Leo could not wait for school.

Leo found a seat near the window and waited for the library to begin. The teacher said Leo's story was not good enough. Leo felt like giving up.
Leo looked out the window on the bus and felt very alone.

It was Monday morning and Ella was happy to go to school.

At school, Ella tried very hard during science class. Ella spilled paint on the floor and the teacher frowned all day.
When Ella got home, Ella sat in the corner and said nothing.

Lily was playing in the garden when Lily saw a ball lying in the grass.

The ball had sharp teeth and made a low growling sound. Lily stepped back quickly. The ball rolled after Lily no matter which way Lily ran. Lily screamed and ran as fast as Lily could.

That night, Lily told a grown-up. Lily's mum said, "Stay far away from the ball. It is not safe." Lily never went back to the garden.

One afternoon, Tom went to the park and found a ball near the fence.

When Tom looked closer, Tom saw the ball had a terrible smell and its surface pulsed like something alive. Tom froze. Ben came to help but stopped when Ben saw the ball. "Ben, run!" Tom shouted. Every time Tom stopped, the ball came closer.

Back inside, Tom told the family everything. Tom's dad shook his head. "That ball is dangerous," he said quietly. Tom could not sleep.

Ben was walking through the backyard when Ben noticed a ball on the ground.

The ball had eyes that glowed red in the dark. Ben's heart began to pound. "Zoe, look out!" cried Ben. Zoe saw the ball and screamed. The ball moved on its own and would not stop following Ben.

Ben locked the door and told Ben's parents. Grandma looked frightened. "Keep away from the ball," she whispered. The ball was never touched again.

It was a quiet day in the field until Zoe spotted a ball by the path.

Something was wrong. The ball had a mouth full of needles that snapped open and shut and Zoe felt very afraid. Zoe called for Sam but Zoe could not stop running. Wherever Zoe went, the ball was right behind.

Later, Zoe whispered what happened to a grown-up. The neighbour saw it too. "Do not touch the ball," she warned. Zoe stayed inside for days.

Sam had never seen the ball before. It was just sitting there in the schoolyard.

Sam saw right away that the ball had cold wet skin like a dead fish. Sam wanted to run. Sam and Mia both tried to get away. The ball blocked every path Sam tried to take. They ran all the way home.

Sam sat shaking and told someone what had happened. Sam's older sister grabbed Sam's hand. "We must never go near the ball again," she said. Sam did not want to go outside again.

Mia was playing in the beach when Mia saw a ball lying in the grass.

The ball had long claws that scraped the ground. Mia stepped back quickly. The ball rolled after Mia no matter which way Mia ran. Mia screamed and ran as fast as Mia could.

That night, Mia told a grown-up. Mia's mum said, "Stay far away from the ball. It is not safe." Mia never went back to the beach.

One afternoon, Jake went to the street and found a ball near the fence.

When Jake looked closer, Jake saw the ball had a horrible screech whenever anyone came near. Jake froze. Ella came to help but stopped when Ella saw the ball. "Ella, run!" Jake shouted. Every time Jake stopped, the ball came closer.

Back inside, Jake told the family everything. Jake's dad shook his head. "That ball is dangerous," he said quietly. Jake could not sleep.

Ella was walking through the playground when Ella noticed a ball on the ground.

The ball had thick black slime dripping from it. Ella's heart began to pound. "Max, look out!" cried Ella. Max saw the ball and screamed. The ball moved on its own and would not stop following Ella.

Ella locked the door and told Ella's parents. Grandma looked frightened. "Keep away from the ball," she whispered. The ball was never touched again.

It was a quiet day in the forest path until Max spotted a ball by the path.

Something was wrong. The ball had spikes all around it that grew longer as it moved and Max felt very afraid. Max called for Lucy but Max could not stop running. Wherever Max went, the ball was right behind.

Later, Max whispered what happened to a grown-up. The neighbour saw it too. "Do not touch the ball," she warned. Max stayed inside for days.

Lucy had never seen the ball before. It was just sitting there in the empty lot.

Lucy saw right away that the ball had a thundering rumble that shook the ground. Lucy wanted to run. Lucy and Leo both tried to get away. The ball blocked every path Lucy tried to take. They ran all the way home.

Lucy sat shaking and told someone what had happened. Lucy's older sister grabbed Lucy's hand. "We must never go near the ball again," she said. Lucy did not want to go outside again.

The ball had sharp teeth and made a low growling sound. Leo stepped back quickly. The ball rolled after Leo no matter which way Leo ran. Leo screamed and ran as fast as Leo could.

That night, Leo told a grown-up. Leo's mum said, "Stay far away from the ball. It is not safe." Leo never went back to the garden.

When Ruby looked closer, Ruby saw the ball had a terrible smell and its surface pulsed like something alive. Ruby froze. Owen came to help but stopped when Owen saw the ball. "Owen, run!" Ruby shouted. Every time Ruby stopped, the ball came closer.

Back inside, Ruby told the family everything. Ruby's dad shook his head. "That ball is dangerous," he said quietly. Ruby could not sleep.

Owen was walking through the backyard when Owen noticed a ball on the ground.

The ball had eyes that glowed red in the dark. Owen's heart began to pound. "Chloe, look out!" cried Owen. Chloe saw the ball and screamed. The ball moved on its own and would not stop following Owen.

Owen locked the door and told Owen's parents. Grandma looked frightened. "Keep away from the ball," she whispered. The ball was never touched again.

It was a quiet day in the field until Chloe spotted a ball by the path.

Something was wrong. The ball had a mouth full of needles that snapped open and shut and Chloe felt very afraid. Chloe called for Kai but Chloe could not stop running. Wherever Chloe went, the ball was right behind.

That night, Lily told a grown-up. Lily's mum said, "Stay far away from the ball. It is not safe." Lily never went back to the garden.

One afternoon, Tom went to the park and found a ball near the fence.

When Tom looked closer, Tom saw the ball had a terrible smell and its surface pulsed like something alive. Tom froze. Mia came to help but stopped when Mia saw the ball. "Mia, run!" Tom shouted. Every time Tom stopped, the ball came closer.

Back inside, Tom told the family everything. Tom's dad shook his head. "That ball is dangerous," he said quietly. Tom could not sleep.

Ben was walking through the backyard when Ben noticed a ball on the ground.

The ball had eyes that glowed red in the dark. Ben's heart began to pound. "Jake, look out!" cried Ben. Jake saw the ball and screamed. The ball moved on its own and would not stop following Ben.

Ben locked the door and told Ben's parents. Grandma looked frightened. "Keep away from the ball," she whispered. The ball was never touched again.

It was a quiet day in the field until Zoe spotted a ball by the path.

Something was wrong. The ball had a mouth full of needles that snapped open and shut and Zoe felt very afraid. Zoe called for Ella but Zoe could not stop running. Wherever Zoe went, the ball was right behind.

Later, Zoe whispered what happened to a grown-up. The neighbour saw it too. "Do not touch the ball," she warned. Zoe stayed inside for days.

Sam had never seen the ball before. It was just sitting there in the schoolyard.

Sam saw right away that the ball had cold wet skin like a dead fish. Sam wanted to run. Sam and Max both tried to get away. The ball blocked every path Sam tried to take. They ran all the way home.

Sam sat shaking and told someone what had happened. Sam's older sister grabbed Sam's hand. "We must never go near the ball again," she said. Sam did not want to go outside again.

Mia was playing in the beach when Mia saw a ball lying in the grass.

The ball had long claws that scraped the ground. Mia stepped back quickly. The ball rolled after Mia no matter which way Mia ran. Mia screamed and ran as fast as Mia could.

That night, Mia told a grown-up. Mia's mum said, "Stay far away from the ball. It is not safe." Mia never went back to the beach.

One afternoon, Jake went to the street and found a ball near the fence.

When Jake looked closer, Jake saw the ball had a horrible screech whenever anyone came near. Jake froze. Leo came to help but stopped when Leo saw the ball. "Leo, run!" Jake shouted. Every time Jake stopped, the ball came closer.

Back inside, Jake told the family everything. Jake's dad shook his head. "That ball is dangerous," he said quietly. Jake could not sleep.

Ella was walking through the playground when Ella noticed a ball on the ground.

The ball had thick black slime dripping from it. Ella's heart began to pound. "Ruby, look out!" cried Ella. Ruby saw the ball and screamed. The ball moved on its own and would not stop following Ella.

Ella locked the door and told Ella's parents. Grandma looked frightened. "Keep away from the ball," she whispered. The ball was never touched again.

It was a quiet day in the forest path until Max spotted a ball by the path.

Something was wrong. The ball had spikes all around it that grew longer as it moved and Max felt very afraid. Max called for Owen but Max could not stop running. Wherever Max went, the ball was right behind.

Later, Max whispered what happened to a grown-up. The neighbour saw it too. "Do not touch the ball," she warned. Max stayed inside for days.

Lucy had never seen the ball before. It was just sitting there in the empty lot.

Lucy saw right away that the ball had a thundering rumble that shook the ground. Lucy wanted to run. Lucy and Chloe both tried to get away. The ball blocked every path Lucy tried to take. They ran all the way home.

Lucy sat shaking and told someone what had happened. Lucy's older sister grabbed Lucy's hand. "We must never go near the ball again," she said. Lucy did not want to go outside again.
